title: NOC 12112 – Records Management Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Records Management Technicians play a vital role in maintaining, classifying, protecting, and retrieving organizational information across Canada’s public and private sectors. Classified under NOC 12112, this occupation benefits from steady post-pandemic demand and offers viable immigration pathways toward Permanent Residence (PR) through Canada’s skilled worker programs and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations
classification.minor_group: 1211 – Court reporters, transcriptionists, records management technicians, and statistical officers

common_job_titles: Information management technician Microfilm records searcher Records classifier Records digitizing technician Records technician
duties: Implement and update records classification, retention, and disposal schedules
Classify, code, cross-reference, log, and store records
Develop document inventories and maintain indexing systems
Operate retrieval systems to research and extract records
Prepare and transfer files according to records life-cycle procedures
Maintain access lists for security-classified documents
Compile statistics and prepare service activity reports
employment_requirements: Completion of a two- to three-year college program in information or records management technology
Two to three years of experience in a records management environment may be required
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 10,500 new positions for Records Management Technicians, compared with 10,600 job seekers—making strategic province selection and immigration planning essential for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Records Management Technicians are employed across government agencies, healthcare institutions, corporations, legal offices, and consulting firms nationwide.

Continued hiring in public administration and corporate compliance unitsGrowing demand for digital records management and archiving specialistsEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported work permit optionsAnnual earnings typically ranging from $38,500 to $82,500 CAD
